attributes.content:
## Overview

The Syndicated Message card gives the workspace a structured home for a message that can be templated and sent across multiple channels (email, chat, SMS). Tracks delivery status per channel.

As a first-class Boxel card, it can be linked to adjacent records, surfaced in filtered views, and reused in automation without the information collapsing into disconnected notes or one-off documents.

## Core Mechanics

Syndicated Message usually evolves through drafting, review, and reuse. The card keeps its core content structured enough to be surfaced in different views, linked from related records, and updated by people or automation without losing provenance.

The card can be created directly by a user, produced from a [[Workflow Playbook]], or updated in response to a [[Trigger]]. When AI is involved, a [[Prompt Template]] or [[Skill Card]] can shape how it is drafted, summarized, classified, or routed.

## Integration Points

- **[[Contact]]** -- Syndicated Message often needs a person-level reference for ownership, communication, or accountability.
- **[[Organization]]** -- Syndicated Message frequently sits inside an account, company, or team-level relationship context.
- **[[Workflow Playbook]]** -- Syndicated Message can be created, reviewed, or progressed as one step inside a repeatable workflow.
- **[[Trigger]]** -- Changes to Syndicated Message can fire automation, and automation can also create or update Syndicated Message.
- **[[Task]]** -- Syndicated Message often creates follow-up work or is itself tracked through task execution.
- **[[Email Template]]** -- Email Template can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Syndicated Message.

## Example Use Cases

- **System of record:** Teams create Syndicated Message as the structured home for a message that can be templated and sent across multiple channels (email, chat, SMS). Tracks delivery status per channel and keep related work linked instead of scattered across notes.
- **Automation handoff:** A [[Workflow Playbook]] or [[Trigger]] creates or updates Syndicated Message when upstream conditions are met, reducing repetitive setup work.
- **Search and reporting:** Syndicated Message stays queryable for dashboards, summaries, and downstream actions without re-entering the same information in multiple places.

cardInfo.name: Syndicated Message
cardInfo.summary: A message that can be templated and sent across multiple channels (email, chat, SMS). Tracks delivery status per channel.
